channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Nick Reiner pleads not guilty to first-degree murder charges in deaths of Rob Reiner and Michele Singer Reiner
item.description: Nick Reiner, the 32-year-old son of acclaimed actor and director , has pleaded not guilty to two counts of first-degree murder following the fatal stabbing of his parents, Rob Reiner and Michele Singe...

item.title: Nat Fraser’s Conviction and the Ongoing Impact of Arlene Fraser’s Disappearance
item.description: Nearly 30 years after Arlene Fraser disappeared from her home in Elgin, Scotland, her body has never been found, yet her husband, Nat Fraser, was twice convicted of her murder. Arlene vanished on 28 A...

item.title: Iranian Drone Strike on RAF Base in Cyprus Confirmed by Ministry of Defence
item.description: The Ministry of Defence has confirmed that a suspected Iranian drone strike took place at the RAF Akrotiri base in Cyprus. The incident occurred at midnight local time, and while no casualties were re...

item.title: Student Protests Expand Across Iranian Universities Following Deadly Crackdown
item.description: A new wave of anti-government protests has erupted across multiple Iranian universities following their reopening after a temporary suspension of in-person classes. The demonstrations, which began on ...

item.title: Tens of thousands of passengers stranded as Middle East airports shut down due to Iranian airstrikes
item.description: Iran's retaliatory airstrikes in the Middle East have caused massive travel disruptions, with airports in Dubai, Doha, and other Gulf nations closing for a second day. More than 3,400 flights have bee...

item.title: Lord Peter Mandelson Arrested Amid Investigation into Alleged Misuse of Government Information
item.description: Lord Peter Mandelson, a senior UK political figure, was arrested on Monday at his Camden residence on suspicion of misconduct in public office. The Metropolitan Police, acting after executing search w...

item.title: PinkPantheress becomes first woman to win Brit Award for Producer of the Year
item.description: Pop star PinkPantheress has been named Producer of the Year at the Brit Awards, becoming the first woman to receive the prize since it was introduced in 1977. At 24, she is also the youngest recipient...

item.title: Two agricultural students jailed for killing sheep with explosives in East Sussex
item.description: Two agricultural students have been sentenced to prison after killing a sheep in a violent and disturbing attack in East Sussex. Leighton Ashby, 22, from Ashford, Kent, and Oakley Hollands, 20, from H...

item.title: Significant Reforms to UK's Asylum System Coming Into Effect
item.description: Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood is set to announce that from Monday, any asylum seeker granted refugee status in the UK will only receive temporary protection, marking one of the most significant chang...

item.title: Former Care Home Manager Found to Have Abused Children Over Nearly Two Decades
item.description: A court has determined that Malcolm Phillips, a former manager of Skircoat Lodge Care Home in Halifax, sexually abused vulnerable children over an 18-year period between 1976 and 1994. Now aged 93 and...

item.title: Microsoft Names AI Executive Asha Sharma to Lead Xbox Following Phil Spencer’s Retirement
item.description: Microsoft has announced a major leadership change in its gaming division, with long-time Xbox chief Phil Spencer retiring after nearly four decades at the company. Spencer, who began as an intern in 1...

item.title: US-Israel Conflict Escalates: Hezbollah Retaliates as Tensions Mount Following Khamenei's Assassination
item.description: The ongoing conflict between the US and Israel against Iran has reached a critical juncture, with Hezbollah claiming responsibility for launching missiles and drones from Lebanon towards Israel. The a...

item.title: Trump signals willingness to negotiate with Iran amid escalating conflict
item.description: US President Donald Trump has expressed his readiness to engage with Iran's surviving leadership as the situation in the Middle East continues to escalate. Following the killing of Iran's Supreme Lead...

item.title: Nick Reiner Pleads Not Guilty to Charges of Murdering His Parents in Los Angeles
item.description: Nick Reiner, the 32-year-old son of acclaimed Hollywood director Rob Reiner, has pleaded not guilty to charges that he murdered his parents, Rob Reiner, 78, and Michele Singer Reiner, 70. The plea was...

item.title: Lindsey Vonn details severe leg injury and recovery after Winter Olympics crash
item.description: American skiing legend Lindsey Vonn has revealed the severity of her injury sustained during the Winter Olympics in Cortina, Italy. At age 41, Vonn suffered a major crash in the women's downhill event...

item.title: Benoît Richaud's Viral Jacket-Switching Moment at the 2026 Winter Olympics
item.description: Benoît Richaud, a French Olympic figure skating choreographer, has become a viral sensation at the 2026 Milano Cortina Winter Games for his unique habit of changing jackets every time one of his 16 sk...

item.title: Polymarket Sees $529 Million Traded on Speculative Bets Regarding U.S. Strike on Iran
item.description: Polymarket, a prediction market platform, experienced an unprecedented $529 million in trades based on bets surrounding the timing of a potential U.S. and Israeli military strike on Iran. According to...

item.title: Lord Mandelson Arrested on Suspicion of Misconduct Linked to Jeffrey Epstein
item.description: Lord Peter Mandelson, a former UK Labour minister and US ambassador, has been arrested on suspicion of misconduct in public office. The investigation follows allegations that Mandelson shared market-s...

item.title: Escalating Middle East Conflict: Israeli Airstrikes on Hezbollah Targets After Retaliation
item.description: Tensions in the Middle East have escalated significantly following the killing of Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei by US and Israeli forces. Hezbollah, a Lebanese militia backed by Iran, ...

item.title: Court administrative error leads to release of remanded rape suspect who leaves the UK
item.description: A man in his forties from London, charged with multiple counts of rape, sexual assault and violence against a woman, was mistakenly released from HMP Wormwood Scrubs due to a court administrative erro...
